svg.title: ARIA system architecture diagram
svg.desc: Three concurrent processes send resource requests to the ARIA agent, which contains a Fingerprinter, Negotiator, and Loan Broker. ARIA sends managed allocation calls down to the OS kernel, which contains a Resource Allocator and Memory Manager.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What happens inside Process P1 when it requests a resource that is already held by another process?')
sendPrompt('What happens inside Process P2 when it requests a resource that is already held by another process?')
sendPrompt('What happens inside Process P3 when it requests a resource that is already held by another process?')
sendPrompt('How does the ARIA Fingerprinter learn deadlock-prone request patterns and predict circular waits before they form?')
sendPrompt('How does the ARIA Negotiator resolve resource conflicts by rescheduling locks — without ever killing a process?')
sendPrompt('How does the Memory Loan Broker temporarily migrate memory pages between processes to break a circular wait?')
sendPrompt('How does the OS resource allocator grant, queue, and deny process lock requests?')
sendPrompt('How does the OS memory manager handle page tables and virtual memory allocation?')
